_meta.title: Station Arrival Beats
_meta.description: Schema A beats fired on station arrival. Each beat has 2-3 choices with deltas. Choices route to crew_meetup stubs (Phase 3c).
_meta._comment: // Phase 3c: crew_meetup_at_<station>_1 stubs will be implemented when mission board lands

station_arrival_KASHNER_01.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_KASHNER_01.text: Kashner Iceworks resolves out of the dark — mountainous blocks of ice held in containment below the ship, docking bays marking the thin hull like stitch-work. Three thousand contractors live here. The air tastes of corporate filtration. A clerk reads your genship_id once, twice, then stamps a fuel chit without looking up.

choices.label: Pay standard rate. Fuel +25.
Ask where the water comes from.
Refuel and skip the receipt.

station_arrival_BENTIC_02.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_BENTIC_02.text: Bentic Penal's hull is grey and featureless — a private prison funded by multiple governments that don't want to know the details. The docking clamp catches with a sound like a cell door. A Bentic Security officer in corporate-military livery checks your invoice-code and doesn't smile. Behind him, through the airlock glass, you can see a yard where conscripts in jumpsuits move in lines.

choices.label: File your cargo. Don't look.
Ask if you can see the labour logs.
Pay the transit fee. Leave.

station_arrival_CORVALLO_03.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_CORVALLO_03.text: Corvallo Station is the busiest hub in the system — privately owned, veiled as neutral ground, and the one place where every faction's invoice is honoured without interrogation. The docking bay is a crossroads: NAC haulers, ED blazers, RRA military patches, Coalition shoulder patches you don't recognise. Three crews at a table. They look up when your genship_id appears on the board.

choices.label: Dock at the public bay. Standard rate.
Ask for the private traders' deck.
Find the three crews. Buy a round.

station_arrival_SXHALO_04.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_SXHALO_04.text: SX Halo is beautiful — a halo-shaped station in permanent orbit around Mars, its curve catching the planet's light like a promise. SX Corp's logo marks every surface. The docking bay is immaculate. The air smells of nothing at all, which is the smell of money. A receptionist with a smile that doesn't reach her eyes welcomes you to the future of human advancement.

choices.label: Accept the corporate hospitality.
Decline. You don't trust clean air.
Ask what SX does here. Directly.

station_arrival_ORPHEUM_05.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_ORPHEUM_05.text: Orpheum Astrogrowth is green — grow-lights baking the arrival deck, the smell of soil and nutrient solution so thick you taste it. The largest private astrofarm ever constructed. A woman with dirt under her nails and a corporate badge waves you through. The Coalition genship grows food. Orpheum grows more. You eat Orpheum produce whether you choose to or not.

choices.label: Accept the tour. See the yields.
Ask about the Coalition crop failures.
Trade fuel for fresh supplies. Fair.

station_arrival_PROPHET_06.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_PROPHET_06.text: Prophet's Threshold docks in rhythm — the clamps sync to a call you feel in your chest before you hear it. A host in clean linen offers water before docking confirmation. The water is cold. The offer is not optional. Around you, conscripts outnumber contractors three to one — this is one of the few stations where that's true.

choices.label: Accept the water. Accept the welcome.
Decline politely. Stay on schedule.
Ask who funded the accelerated launch.

station_arrival_DENISE_07.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_DENISE_07.text: Denise Mar's arrival hall is quiet in the way NAC stations are quiet — deliberate, fair, and watching. Her portrait hangs above the intake desk: a conscript woman who negotiated the fairest labour-truce in belt history. The conscript beside the portrait smiles at you like a host, not a gatekeeper. The station still honours her terms.

choices.label: Ask for the conscript discount. It exists.
Donate to the hall. Take nothing.
Ask what happened to Denise Mar.

station_arrival_BEREZINA_08.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_BEREZINA_08.text: Berezina Drift is a patchwork — conspot welding over conspot welding, each layer a different crew's desperate repair. The EU's frontier expansion failed here. The government funding dried up. The crew who were supposed to man it were reassigned. What remains is a skeleton crew of conscripts who stayed because they had nowhere else to go. The cargo master doesn't ask for your invoice. She asks what you brought.

choices.label: Trade fuel for hull patches.
Offer cash. She doesn't want it.
Ask who's still running this place.

station_arrival_MOSCOW_09.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_MOSCOW_09.text: Moscow Prospekt's docking bay is clean enough to eat from — and someone probably does. An officer in pressed uniform meets you at the airlock. He doesn't salute. He assesses. Behind him, through the corridor glass, you can see the officers' mess on one side and the conscript paperwork hall on the other. The two rooms share a wall and nothing else.

choices.label: Stand at attention. Protocol first.
Offer a drink. Break the ice.
Ask about the flu program. Carefully.

station_arrival_CORAL_10.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_CORAL_10.text: Coral station is a wheel of union halls and transit authority offices. The belt's only place where genship crews mix without a handler. Your invoice-code gets you through the gate. The crowd reads it and knows what you are. Three crews at a table. They're talking about your predecessor. They stop when you sit down.

choices.label: Join the table. Introduce yourself.
Nod and move past. Listen later.
Ask who's watching this station.

station_arrival_KASHNER_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_KASHNER_11.text: Kashner Iceworks on a shift change. The docking queue is twice as long — three haulers ahead of you, all running water contracts. A dockhand waves you to the overflow bay. The overflow bay doesn't have fuel lines. You'll have to jerrycan it.

choices.label: Wait for the main bay. Burn hold time.
Take the overflow. Jerrycan fuel.
Ask the dockhand about the haulers.

station_arrival_BENTIC_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_BENTIC_11.text: Bentic Penal's outer ring is quiet — too quiet. A third of the docking clamps are locked open, empty. The security officer who met you last time isn't on rotation. The one who is doesn't check your invoice. She just waves you through. Something changed since your last visit.

choices.label: Dock. Watch the corridors carefully.
Ask what happened to the missing clamps.
Fuel and leave. Fast turnaround.

station_arrival_CORVALLO_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_CORVALLO_11.text: Corvallo's trade floor is in lock-down — the private deck is closed, shutters down, armed guards at the freight lift. A notice on the public board reads: inventory audit. The crews at the tables aren't talking. They're counting what they have left.

choices.label: Dock at public bay. Wait it out.
Find a crew who knows what happened.
Leave. Come back next transit.

station_arrival_SXHALO_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_SXHALO_11.text: SX Halo's reception is different this time. No smile. The airlock queue moves fast — too fast, like they want you processed and gone. A technician in a sealed suit crosses the docking bay carrying a sample container marked with biohazard bands. He doesn't make eye contact.

choices.label: Follow standard docking. Don't stare.
Ask about the biohazard container.
Refuel from the external pump only.

station_arrival_ORPHEUM_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_ORPHEUM_11.text: Orpheum's grow-lights are dimmed — not off, but cycling at a fraction of their usual intensity. The woman with dirt under her nails isn't at the dock. Her replacement is clean-handed and won't explain. The produce shipment you were promised isn't on the loading manifest.

choices.label: Ask what happened to the grow schedule.
Take what's available. Adapt.
Leave. You'll source elsewhere.

station_arrival_PROPHET_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_PROPHET_11.text: Prophet's Threshold is in mourning — black cloth draped over the arrival hall arches, the rhythm of the docking clamps slower than usual. A station attendant whispers that a conscript crew died in a pressure failure three shifts ago. The water is still offered. It's offered harder now.

choices.label: Accept the water. Stay for the funeral.
Ask about the pressure failure.
Refuel quietly. Don't intrude.

station_arrival_DENISE_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_DENISE_11.text: Denise Mar's hall has a new portrait beside the old one — a younger face, a recent death. The conscript at the desk tells you it's the station founder's granddaughter. She kept the labour-truce alive after her grandmother couldn't. The station is still debating whether to honour her terms or renegotiate.

choices.label: Honour the old terms. Pay full.
Ask who's pushing the renegotiation.
Dock. Stay out of it.

station_arrival_BEREZINA_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_BEREZINA_11.text: Someone's been welding on Berezina Drift since your last visit — new plating over the worst sections, actual alloy instead of cargo wrap. The cargo master has a new suit. She won't say where the materials came from. The station smells less like desperation and more like ambition.

choices.label: Ask who's funding the repairs.
Trade for the new plating. You need it.
Refuel. Don't ask questions.

station_arrival_MOSCOW_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_MOSCOW_11.text: Moscow Prospekt's sick bay is full. You can see it through the corridor glass — patients on every bunk, medics moving between them without stopping. The officer who meets you has a cough he's suppressing. The quarantine notices are posted, but no one is enforcing them at the dock.

choices.label: Dock. Keep your crew on the ship.
Offer medical supplies if you have them.
Ask about the outbreak. Report it.

station_arrival_CORAL_11.speaker: narrator
station_arrival_CORAL_11.text: Coral's union hall has a petition on the board — crew conditions, transit fees, the usual. But this one has signatures from four different genship affiliations. That hasn't happened before. The table where crews used to talk is empty. They're at the petition board instead.

choices.label: Read the petition. Add your signature.
Copy the petition. File it privately.
Fuel and leave. Not your fight.
